purpose: Formal V5-MOD-1 Phase 4 perf-gate result. Compares the post-Phase-3 dispatch (Box<dyn ScoringSource> via factory registry) against the V5-FOUND-1 baseline ceiling p95_ms ≤ 19. Captured 2026-05-02 same host class as the baseline.

captured_against.host: Windows 11 Home, MSI laptop, 8 GB RAM (same host class as the baseline)
captured_against.build_profile: debug (cargo build, dev profile, unoptimized + debuginfo) — matches baseline build profile

ceiling_rule: p95_ms ≤ 19 (5% above baseline p95=18, rounded up)
verdict_summary: Box<dyn ScoringSource> dispatch + #[async_trait] future-boxing per domain produces a reproducible p95 regression of +6 to +10 ms over the V5-FOUND-1 baseline. All three independent 30-sample captures exceed the p95_ms ≤ 19 ceiling. The plan's 🔴 BLOCKING perf-gate fires; Theme B halts pending architectural decision.

regression_diagnosis.likely_dominant_factor: async_trait future-boxing — every ScoringSource::load(...) call allocates a Pin<Box<dyn Future>> via the #[async_trait] macro. Across 19 domains per scoring run, that's 19 heap allocations + 19 dynamic dispatches that the v4 string-match dispatch did NOT do (the v4 path inlined each arm).
secondary_factors: Per-domain HashMap lookup on registry.get(source_type) (~50ns × 19 = ~1µs total)
Per-domain virtual dispatch on source.load(...) (~10ns × 19 = ~200ns total)
regression_diagnosis.absolute_magnitude: p95 regression is ~6 ms over a ~18 ms baseline. In absolute terms scoring is still under 30 ms p95. Real-world impact is unmeasurable for typical scoring cadence (<1 Hz). The gate fails on RELATIVE terms (5% threshold), not on production-meaningfulness.

fallback_options.label: Implement plan-documented fallback: generic-bounded + small enum for built-ins
fallback_options.what: Define `enum BuiltinScoringSource { Cmdb, A2a, Function }` with inlined `match` dispatch. Built-ins skip Box<dyn> entirely. The `ScoringSource` trait remains for third-party impls (which pay the dyn cost when used).
fallback_options.tradeoff: Two dispatch paths (built-in vs third-party). Adds enum maintenance burden. Closes the gate fully (built-ins go through inlined enum; zero overhead).

fallback_options.label: Cache built sources in the registry (HashMap<&str, Box<dyn ScoringSource>>)
fallback_options.what: Keep current architecture; eliminate the per-call factory.build() Box::new by caching built sources. Future-boxing on load() calls remains.
fallback_options.tradeoff: Smaller refactor. Likely closes ~30% of the gap (eliminates Box::new but not future-boxing). Probably NOT enough to pass the strict 5% ceiling — likely brings p95 from 24-28 down to 21-25. Still fails.

fallback_options.label: Synchronous trait split: SyncScoringSource + AsyncScoringSource
fallback_options.what: Built-ins (cmdb + function) implement a sync trait (no future-boxing). A2A implements an async trait (boxed; pays the cost when used). Two-trait architecture; registry holds Box<dyn> of either kind.
fallback_options.tradeoff: Cleaner than option A in some ways (no enum to maintain). But complicates the trait surface (callers need to know which trait their source impls). Equivalent to A in perf — built-ins skip the future-boxing.

fallback_options.label: Accept the regression with documented operator justification
fallback_options.what: The 5% ceiling was conservative; the absolute regression (+6ms p95) is unmeasurable for real scoring cadence (<1 Hz). Document the trade-off, mark the gate as 'waived per operator pin', proceed to V5-MOD-2/3 with the current Box<dyn> architecture.
fallback_options.tradeoff: Clean modularity, simple architecture, but breaks the strict adversary BLOCKING gate. Sets precedent for future perf-gate waivers. The gate exists to prevent unbounded slow drift; waiving on the FIRST failure invites future creep.

recommendation: Operator pin required. Plan-critic's 🔴 BLOCKING posture suggests Option A (the plan-documented fallback) — it's the path the plan explicitly named when authoring the gate. Options B/C are alternatives; D is a deliberate gate waiver. Plan recommends NOT-D unless real-world latency budgets demand simplicity (which they don't here — scoring is sub-30ms even with the regression).

cross_references.phase_3_commit: c7afaa1 v5 V5-MOD-1 Phase 3: route 3 dispatch sites through ScoringSourceRegistry
